title: matthiola conferta
intro: Matthiola conferta, also known as the Western stock or horned stock, is a biennial flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and fever, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Matthiola conferta include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and soothe sore throats. The herbal preparations of Matthiola conferta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent.
